resultRaw.sql:
SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM st.date_of_birth)::INT AS birth_year, COUNT(*) AS student_count
FROM public.students AS st
WHERE st.date_of_birth IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM st.date_of_birth)::INT
ORDER BY birth_year;

issues: Visualization encodes both fields present in metadata (birth_year, student_count) — OK.
SQL groups by birth_year and the chart aggregates by birth_year (bar heights = student_count) — OK.

The x encoding uses type = "ordinal" for birth_year, but birth_year is a numeric year (EXTRACT(YEAR)::INT). Consider using a temporal or quantitative type so ordering/spacing is numeric/time-based rather than purely categorical.
Using "sort":"ascending" with an ordinal axis can lead to lexicographic ordering; ensure the sort is numeric if keeping ordinal.
Title and axis labels align with the question intent — OK.

result.sql:
SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM st.date_of_birth)::INT AS birth_year, COUNT(*) AS student_count
FROM public.students AS st
WHERE st.date_of_birth IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM st.date_of_birth)::INT
ORDER BY birth_year;

issues: Visualization encodes both fields present in metadata (birth_year, student_count) — OK.
SQL groups by birth_year and the chart aggregates by birth_year (bar heights = student_count) — OK.

The x encoding uses type = "ordinal" for birth_year, but birth_year is a numeric year (EXTRACT(YEAR)::INT). Consider using a temporal or quantitative type so ordering/spacing is numeric/time-based rather than purely categorical.
Using "sort":"ascending" with an ordinal axis can lead to lexicographic ordering; ensure the sort is numeric if keeping ordinal.
Title and axis labels align with the question intent — OK.
